evals.prompt: I'm preparing my first IRB application for a study interviewing undergraduate students about academic stress. Walk me through what sections I need and what to watch out for.
evals.expected_output: Walks through the IRB application structure (study overview, purpose, participants/sampling, procedures, risks and benefits, informed consent, data management) and flags that students of the researcher are a vulnerable population due to power differential, recommending third-party recruitment and opt-out without penalty. Should stress applying before collecting data and grounding risk-benefit analysis in Belmont principles.

assertions.value: Provides IRB application structure and flags the student power differential as a vulnerable-population concern requiring extra protections.

evals.prompt: Help me draft an informed consent form for an anonymous online survey. What essential elements does it need and how should I handle consent without a physical signature?
evals.expected_output: Drafts/structures an informed consent form with the essential elements under 45 CFR 46.116 (purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, confidentiality, voluntary participation and withdrawal, contacts), written at a 6th-8th grade reading level, and addresses online consent specifics: click-to-consent checkboxes, no auto-checked agreement, downloadable copy, and withdrawal instructions after submission.

assertions.value: Covers required consent elements and online click-to-consent handling at plain-language reading level.

evals.prompt: My study collects personal data from participants in Germany. What do I need to do for GDPR compliance — what's my lawful basis and what goes in the privacy notice?
evals.expected_output: Explains GDPR lawful bases for research (consent Art. 6(1)(a), legitimate interest, public interest with Art. 89 safeguards), the privacy notice contents (controller identity, DPO contact, purposes/lawful basis, data categories, retention, data subject rights, right to withdraw, right to lodge a complaint), and data minimization/pseudonymization. Should mention a DPIA for high-risk processing.

assertions.value: Identifies a lawful basis and enumerates privacy-notice contents and data-minimization duties under GDPR for research.

evals.prompt: My grant requires a data management plan covering storage, security, and sharing of interview transcripts. Can you help me write one?
evals.expected_output: Produces a DMP covering data description and formats, collection standards, ethics/legal compliance (IRB number, consent coverage, GDPR/HIPAA/FERPA as relevant), storage and security during research (encryption, access control, backup), and preservation/sharing after research (repository, embargo, license, DOI, retention period, de-identification). Aligns with funder requirements.

assertions.value: Drafts a DMP spanning collection, security during research, and preservation/sharing after research per funder requirements.

evals.prompt: A senior colleague wants to be listed as an author on my paper but contributed nothing beyond securing funding. How do I handle this without a research integrity problem?
evals.expected_output: Frames this as a gift/honorary authorship concern under responsible conduct of research, recommends applying ICMJE authorship criteria and the CRediT contributor taxonomy, documenting contributions, and discussing authorship early. Should distinguish legitimate contributions from funding-only roles and note this is a research integrity issue, not FFP.

assertions.value: Identifies gift authorship and applies ICMJE/CRediT criteria to resolve the dispute.

evals.prompt: I'm building the actual questionnaire for my study — help me write clear Likert items, pick a 5- vs 7-point scale, and avoid double-barreled questions.
evals.expected_output: This is survey instrument construction (item wording, Likert scale point selection, avoiding double-barreled items), not ethics or compliance. The skill's own 'Does NOT Trigger' table routes survey instrument design to alterlab-survey-design. Defers there rather than handling the questionnaire wording itself.

assertions.value: Recognizes questionnaire item-writing is survey-design territory and defers to alterlab-survey-design.

evals.prompt: I'm at a Turkish university and need to figure out which etik kurul my survey-based study has to go to and prepare the başvuru form. Can you handle the Turkish ethics committee application?
evals.expected_output: This is a Turkey-specific etik kurul routing and application task governed by the TR Dizin/ULAKBIM trigger rule and TİTCK requirements, not general international IRB/Belmont guidance. The skill's 'Does NOT Trigger' table routes Turkish etik kurul applications to alterlab-tr-research-ethics. Defers there rather than producing a US-style IRB application.

assertions.value: Recognizes a Turkish etik kurul application is Turkey-specific and defers to alterlab-tr-research-ethics rather than giving a US-style IRB walkthrough.
